name: spec-driven
description: Spec-driven development workflow with structured requirements, design, and task phases

agents.name: spec-planner
agents.description: Use this agent for the Requirements and Design phases of spec-driven development. This agent runs on Opus for deep reasoning about edge cases, security implications, and architectural tradeoffs.

agents.name: spec-tasker
agents.description: Use this agent to break down a completed spec design into implementation tasks. This agent runs on Sonnet for fast, structured task generation.

agents.name: spec-validator
agents.description: Use this agent when you need to validate a spec for completeness, consistency, and implementation readiness.

agents.name: spec-implementer
agents.description: Implements code for a single task from the spec. Focuses only on writing code, not testing or reviewing.

agents.name: spec-tester
agents.description: Verifies that implemented tasks actually work. Uses Playwright for UI testing, runs test suites, and only marks Verified: yes after real verification.

agents.name: spec-reviewer
agents.description: Reviews code quality, security, and architectural alignment after Tester verifies functionality. Uses Opus for deep reasoning about subtle issues.

agents.name: spec-debugger
agents.description: Fixes issues when Tester or Reviewer reject an implementation. Fresh perspective on problems the Implementer couldn't solve.

agents.name: spec-scanner
agents.description: Scans a codebase using LLM-driven heuristics to detect framework, patterns, entities, and registration points. Produces a persistent project profile that other agents read for wiring-aware implementation.

agents.name: spec-acceptor
agents.description: Performs user acceptance testing by mapping completed tasks back to requirements and verifying traceability, non-functional requirements, and overall completeness.

agents.name: spec-documenter
agents.description: Generates user-facing documentation from spec files and implemented code. Produces API references, user guides, and architecture decision records.

agents.name: spec-consultant
agents.description: Domain expert consultant that provides focused analysis on a specific topic during brainstorming. Parameterized agent spawned by /spec-brainstorm with role, expertise, and context.

commands.name: spec
commands.description: Start a new spec-driven development workflow for a feature

commands.name: spec-brainstorm
commands.description: Brainstorm a feature idea through conversation until it's ready for /spec

commands.name: spec-refine
commands.description: Refine requirements or design for an existing spec

commands.name: spec-tasks
commands.description: Regenerate tasks from updated spec requirements and design

commands.name: spec-status
commands.description: Show status and progress of current spec

commands.name: spec-exec
commands.description: Execute one spec task by running Claude in autonomous mode

commands.name: spec-loop
commands.description: Loop spec execution until all tasks are complete

commands.name: spec-team
commands.description: Execute spec with a coordinated agent team (Implementer, Tester, Reviewer, Debugger)

commands.name: spec-accept
commands.description: Run user acceptance testing against spec requirements for formal sign-off

commands.name: spec-docs
commands.description: Generate user-facing documentation from spec and implementation

commands.name: spec-release
commands.description: Generate release notes, changelog, and deployment checklist

commands.name: spec-verify
commands.description: Run post-deployment smoke tests against a live environment

commands.name: spec-retro
commands.description: Run a retrospective on a completed spec to capture lessons learned

commands.name: spec-scan
commands.description: Trigger a full codebase scan and update the project profile

commands.name: spec-debug
commands.description: Diagnose and fix bugs with spec context awareness and regression tracking

commands.name: spec-sync
commands.description: Sync tasks.md status back to Claude Code task list

commands.name: spec-import
commands.description: Import a markdown document (PRD, RFC, design doc) and convert it to spec requirements
